Rev. A
VENDOR DATA REQUIREMENT
FOR
BURNER OIL GUN REPLACEMENT
UNIT : CDU-3 (131)
PROJECT : CAPACITY / RELIABILITY
IMPROVEMENT
CLIENT : BPCL-MUMBAI REFINERY
A 05.01.2026 ISSUED FOR PROCUREMENT AS RM SS
Rev. Prepared Checked Approved
Date Purpose
No by by by

Rev.A
VENDOR DATA REQUIREMENTS
The following drawings/documents marked “ ” shall be furnished by the contractor .
POST ORDER
With
Sl. No. Description With Bid For For Data Remarks
Review Record Book
(Final)
Updated General arrangement
drawing of burner along with bill of
1 Note-8
material showing material of
construction of all components.
2 Updated Burner data sheets
Updated Capacity curves showing
3 heat liberation versus fuel oil /
atomising steam pressure
Updated Registered draft loss curve
4 showing combustion air pressure
drop versus heat liberation
5 Signed and stamped Checklist
Updated Installation, operation and
maintenance manual
Detail procedure for implementing
the modifications
List of spares
- Mandatory
8 - Two year
operation (if
recommended)
Inspection Test Plan duly reviewed
9 @-Note-3
by TPI
Preservation Procedure (Precautions
to be taken during transport, storage
at site, installation & post
installation before commissioning)
Notes:
1. All drawings and documents shall be submitted in A4 or A3 paper sizes. Documents in higher
paper size shall be submitted in exceptional circumstances or as indicated in the MR/Tender.
2. Post Order-The schedule of drawing/ data submission shall be mutually agreed between EIL & the
contractor / supplier during finalization of Document Control Index (DCI).
3. ‘@’ denotes submission of documents to inspection Agency. Copy to be provided to EIL.
4. Bill of Material shall form part of the respective drawing.
5. “TICK” denotes applicability.
6. Post order, drawing/document review shall commence only after approval of DCI.
7. All post order documents shall be submitted/ approved through e-mail.

Rev.A
8. All process parameters shall be marked in the general arrangement drawing (GAD). The
dimensioned GAD’s of the burner shall show the air register, guard, air swirlers (if any) with
position of gas nozzles, pilot gas nozzles, burner block, viewing and ignition ports, integral piping,
valves, interlocks, etc. and space required for extracting the fuel oil guns.
